{"idea": {"Name": "cond_adaptive_test_time_scaling", "Title": "Condition-Adaptive Subspace Rank Scheduling for Latency-Bounded Test-Time Scaling", "Short Hypothesis": "The optimal projection rank for zero-training test-time latent optimization is dynamically governed by the condition number of the layer-wise activation covariance matrix. This direction is necessary because static rank allocation either over-perturbs stable representations (wasting wall-clock latency) or under-perturbs entangled ones (degrading accuracy). Autoregressive generation is the ideal setting as it exhibits non-stationary activation geometry, making real-time geometric conditioning essential. Simpler alternatives like fixed heuristics or global PCA fail to capture token-level geometric shifts, while training-based adapters violate zero-training constraints.
